description: Expected Phase-1 (code conversion) output: the consolidated warehouse SQL that feeds every worksheet, plus the calc-field translations. This is the regression target — a fresh agent run's Phase-1 output should match this.
column_names_note: The .twb is a published Virtual Connection, so its physical column names are GUIDs behind the connection. Column names below are the human-readable captions from the datasource metadata, UPPER-cased as a stand-in; a real run resolves the true warehouse names from the published datasource (see connection-mapping.md). What is authoritative here is the LOGIC: which tables, which joins, which aggregation, and each calc-field's SQL.

datasource.model: relationship (single federated data source over the CSA retail star)
datasource.field_resolution_gotcha: "Region" is a caption in BOTH CUSTOMER_DIM and STORE_DIM. The worksheets use the CUSTOMER_DIM one (resolved by internal field id, not caption). A caption-only match would silently pick the wrong table. See gotchas.md (field-name resolution).

Customer Value Tier.tableau: IF [Lifetime Revenue] >= 5000 THEN "Platinum" ELSEIF [Lifetime Revenue] >= 2000 THEN "Gold" ELSEIF [Lifetime Revenue] >= 500 THEN "Silver" ELSE "Bronze" END
Customer Value Tier.sql: CASE WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 5000 THEN 'Platinum' WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 2000 THEN 'Gold' WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 500 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze' END
used_by: Gross Margin % by Customer Value Tier

Ship Speed Category.tableau: IF [Days To Ship] <= 2 THEN "Express (1-2 days)" ELSEIF [Days To Ship] <= 5 THEN "Standard (3-5 days)" ELSEIF [Days To Ship] <= 10 THEN "Economy (6-10 days)" ELSE "Slow (10+ days)" END
Ship Speed Category.sql: CASE WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 2 THEN 'Express (1-2 days)' WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 5 THEN 'Standard (3-5 days)' WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 10 THEN 'Economy (6-10 days)' ELSE 'Slow (10+ days)' END
used_by: Gross Revenue by Ship Speed Category
Return Rate by Ship Speed Category

Gross Margin Pct.tableau: IIF(SUM([Gross Revenue]) != 0, SUM([Gross Profit]) / SUM([Gross Revenue]), 0)
used_by: Gross Margin % by Customer Value Tier
Gross Margin Pct.note: Ratio of two aggregates — computed at the chart's grain. IIF->IFF; keeps Tableau's 0-when-denominator-0 behavior (NULLIF would give NULL instead).

used_by: Return Rate by Ship Speed Category
Return Rate.note: COUNT (not COUNTD). Assumes IS_RETURNED is numeric 0/1; if the warehouse column is BOOLEAN, use SUM(CASE WHEN f.IS_RETURNED THEN 1 ELSE 0 END).

used_by: Order Channel vs Ship Method (pie: Category x COUNTD)
Gross Revenue by Ship Speed Category

YTD Revenue.note: Defined in the data source but not placed on any migrated worksheet. Do NOT build it into the dashboard SQL (build only what's used). Carry it to the data-source guide / semantic layer as the canonical YTD metric. LOD -> window per tableau-semantics.md; [Year] is DATE_DIM.YEAR, so this form also needs the DATE_DIM join, which the migrated charts don't otherwise require.

Promo ROI.tableau: (SUM([Net Revenue]) - SUM([Promo Cost])) / IIF(SUM([Promo Cost]) != 0, SUM([Promo Cost]), 1)
Promo ROI.note: Unused by migrated worksheets; needs the PROMO_DIM join. Carry to the guide, don't build into the dashboard SQL.

clusters.rationale: All 6 migrated worksheets sit on the same star at order-line grain with no static data-source filter, so they consolidate into ONE cell. Build at the finest grain any chart needs (line rows — required for COUNTD(Order Id) and the ratio measures); each chart aggregates from this dataframe in Phase 2. Join only the tables actually used (ORDER_FACT + CUSTOMER_DIM + PRODUCT_DIM).
worksheets: Gross Margin % by Customer Value Tier
Gross Revenue by Ship Speed Category
Return Rate by Ship Speed Category
clusters.sql:
SELECT
  f.ORDER_ID,
  f.GROSS_REVENUE,
  f.GROSS_PROFIT,
  f.NET_REVENUE,
  f.IS_RETURNED,
  f.ORDER_DATE,
  c.REGION,
  p.CATEGORY,
  CASE WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 2 THEN 'Express (1-2 days)'
       WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 5 THEN 'Standard (3-5 days)'
       WHEN f.DAYS_TO_SHIP <= 10 THEN 'Economy (6-10 days)'
       ELSE 'Slow (10+ days)' END AS SHIP_SPEED_CATEGORY,
  CASE WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 5000 THEN 'Platinum'
       WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 2000 THEN 'Gold'
       WHEN c.LIFETIME_REVENUE >= 500 THEN 'Silver'
       ELSE 'Bronze' END AS CUSTOMER_VALUE_TIER
FROM ORDER_FACT f
LEFT JOIN CUSTOMER_DIM c ON f.CUSTOMER_KEY = c.CUSTOMER_KEY
LEFT JOIN PRODUCT_DIM p ON f.PRODUCT_KEY = p.PRODUCT_KEY

Order Channel vs Ship Method.note: Sheet name is stale; the XML encodings are color=Category, size=COUNTD(Order Id) — the .twb is source of truth.

Monthly Revenue Trend.note: date stays a DATE; apply month granularity as the chart truncUnit (see tableau-semantics.md dates rule).

dependent_sql.reason: Ratio-of-aggregates — a Hex EXPLORE/METRIC aggregates ONE column with a built-in aggregation and cannot compute SUM(a)/SUM(b). Pre-aggregate to the chart grain in a thin SQL cell that reads the shared dataframe (no re-query of the base), then plot the ratio column.
dependent_sql.sql:
SELECT CUSTOMER_VALUE_TIER,
       IFF(SUM(GROSS_REVENUE) <> 0, SUM(GROSS_PROFIT) / SUM(GROSS_REVENUE), 0) AS GROSS_MARGIN_PCT
FROM {{orders_line}}
GROUP BY CUSTOMER_VALUE_TIER

Gross Margin % by Customer Value Tier.measure: GROSS_MARGIN_PCT (plot directly; agg Max/Avg over the one row per group = identity)

dependent_sql.sql:
SELECT SHIP_SPEED_CATEGORY,
       SUM(IS_RETURNED) / COUNT(ORDER_ID) AS RETURN_RATE
FROM {{orders_line}}
GROUP BY SHIP_SPEED_CATEGORY

interactivity.note: Every worksheet carries a categorical filter on [Action (Region)] with groupfilter function='level-members' — this is a Tableau dashboard FILTER ACTION (interactive cross-filter), NOT a static data filter. Do NOT emit it as a SQL WHERE clause. In Hex it maps to cross-filtering / an input parameter, handled in the app layer (Phase 2), not the shared SQL.

phase2_flags: RESOLVED (verified against hex-file-schema.json ExploreField/MetricCellConfig): a Hex EXPLORE/METRIC aggregates ONE column with a built-in aggregation (Sum/Avg/Count/CountDistinct/Min/Max/Median/StdDev/Variance) — there is NO per-field formula/expression and NO calculated-column mechanism. Ratio-of-aggregate measures are therefore handled by the companion grouped SQL in clusters[].dependent_sql (which reads the shared dataframe, preserving consolidation), OR by a semantic-model MEASURE (fieldType MEASURE + semanticProjectId; not CLI-authorable today). Additive measures (Sum/Count/CountDistinct) still ride the shared line-grain cell via EXPLORE aggregation.
Monthly Revenue Trend: keep ORDER_DATE as a DATE; set the chart's truncUnit to month, don't TO_CHAR in SQL.
